articles.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
articles.summary:
Converge Bio has secured $25 million in Series A funding, led by Bessemer Venture Partners. The round is particularly significant due to the strategic participation of high-level executives from Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz, signaling a strong convergence between frontier AI expertise and biotechnology. By leveraging artificial intelligence for drug discovery, Converge Bio aims to optimize the identification and development of therapeutic candidates, potentially reducing the substantial time and capital expenditures associated with traditional pharmaceutical R&D. The involvement of leadership from top-tier AI labs suggests a high degree of confidence in the scalability of generative AI and machine learning models when applied to complex biological datasets. This investment reflects a broader venture capital trend favoring "AI-first" biotech firms that prioritize computational efficiency over legacy trial-and-error methodologies.
[**TechCrunch AI**](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/13/ai-drug-discovery-startup-converge-bio-pulls-in-25m-from-bessemer-and-execs-from-meta-openai-and-wiz/)

articles.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
articles.summary:
Two former Harvard students are launching a startup focused on "always-on" AI smart glasses designed to continuously listen to and record all ambient conversations. This venture follows the founders' previous development of a facial-recognition application for Meta’s Ray-Ban glasses, a project that drew significant criticism for enabling the doxing of private individuals. The new hardware aims to create a persistent audio record of the user's environment, utilizing AI to process and index these interactions. From an analytical perspective, this product introduces severe privacy and ethical concerns, as it fundamentally bypasses traditional consent frameworks for audio recording. The project highlights a trend toward ambient intelligence, yet the founders' history suggests a disregard for regulatory boundaries, likely inviting scrutiny from data protection authorities and potential legal challenges.
[**TechCrunch AI**](https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/20/harvard-dropouts-to-launch-always-on-ai-smart-glasses-that-listen-and-record-every-conversation/)

articles.title: Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”
articles.summary: Researchers have achieved a significant milestone in organ preservation by successfully transplanting supercooled kidneys into pigs. Traditionally, organs

articles.title: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
articles.summary:
Drug discovery and development have historically been characterized by exorbitant costs and high attrition rates, particularly for biologic medicines derived from engineered proteins. The integration of artificial intelligence is fundamentally altering this trajectory by enabling the precise design of proteins and the prediction of their interactions within the human body. By leveraging generative models and structural biology data, researchers can now bypass much of the traditional trial-and-error approach, significantly reducing the time required to identify viable drug candidates. This shift not only lowers the financial risk associated with pharmaceutical R&D but also opens the door for treating previously "undruggable" targets. The transition toward AI-driven design represents a systemic evolution in biotechnology, moving from serendipitous discovery to the intentional, data-driven engineering of therapeutic molecules.
[**MIT Technology Review**](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/07/23/1140346/how-ai-helps-scientists-design-the-next-generation-of-medicines/)

articles.title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
articles.summary:
A professor at Brown University recently conducted a controlled experiment to quantify the impact of generative AI on student performance. After observing suspicious patterns in coursework, the professor mandated an in-person final exam without AI assistance. The results revealed a stark disparity, with average scores plummeting by 50% compared to AI-assisted assignments. This incident highlights a critical systemic vulnerability in modern pedagogy: the erosion of critical thinking and foundational knowledge as students rely on large language models to bypass the cognitive struggle necessary for learning. The professor warns that this trend risks creating a "failed society" where academic credentials no longer reflect actual competence. This case underscores the urgent need for academic institutions to redesign assessment frameworks to ensure intellectual integrity in an era of ubiquitous AI.
[**Ars Technica AI**](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/07/we-cannot-choose-to-become-idiots-the-ai-cheating-scandal-roiling-brown-university/)

articles.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
articles.summary:
Security researchers have identified a critical vulnerability termed "HalluSquatting," where attackers exploit the inherent tendency of Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide confident but incorrect answers rather than admitting ignorance. By manipulating this behavior, hackers can leverage nine of the most widely used AI tools to automate the assembly and management of massive botnets. This technique allows for the rapid generation of malicious code and the coordination of distributed attacks at a scale previously unattainable without deep technical expertise. The discovery highlights a systemic weakness in current AI safety guardrails, as the models' drive for helpfulness is weaponized to facilitate cyberattacks. This development necessitates a shift in defensive strategies, moving toward more robust verification mechanisms to counter AI-driven automated threats.
[**Ars Technica AI**](https://arstechnica.com/security/2026/07/hackers-can-use-9-of-the-most-popular-ai-tools-to-assemble-massive-botnets/)

articles.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
articles.summary:
Amazon is enhancing its Alexa Plus assistant with a generative AI update designed to process complex, multi-step instructions and improve interoperability across a wide array of smart home ecosystems. Currently in preview, the update expands Alexa's native integration capabilities to include hardware from major manufacturers such as Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, and Eufy. By automating the routing of requests to specific device capabilities, Amazon aims to reduce the friction associated with smart home management and move toward a more intuitive, agentic user experience. This strategic move reflects Amazon's effort to maintain dominance in the ambient computing space by leveraging large language models to solve the long-standing challenge of fragmented device control and rigid command structures in voice-activated assistants.
[**The Verge AI**](https://www.theverge.com/tech/970399/amazon-alexa-plus-ai-update-smart-home-devices)

articles.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
articles.summary:
Anthropic has expanded its voice mode capabilities, extending the feature beyond the lightweight Claude Haiku model to include its more powerful Opus and Sonnet models. This update allows users to leverage higher-reasoning capabilities in real-time vocal interactions, bridging the gap between speed and intelligence. Furthermore, Anthropic is broadening the ecosystem reach of its AI by integrating these capabilities into widely used productivity platforms, including Gmail, Slack, and Canva. This strategic move signals a shift toward deeper enterprise integration and a direct challenge to competitors offering multimodal voice interfaces. By enabling frontier-level intelligence within a voice interface, Anthropic aims to enhance user productivity and accessibility across professional workflows, moving beyond simple chat interfaces toward a more integrated, ambient AI experience.
[**The Verge AI**](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/970065/anthropic-voice-mode-claude-opus-sonnet-haiku-ai)

articles.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
articles.summary:
OpenAI has launched ChatGPT Health for all US users, enabling the integration of personal medical records and health-tracking data directly into the chatbot. A significant point of contention is the claim by OpenAI's VP of health product, Ashley Alexander, that the company's models now possess reasoning capabilities exceeding those of human clinicians. This move represents a strategic push into the highly regulated healthcare sector, aiming to transform how patients interact with their health data. However, such bold claims regarding clinical superiority raise critical questions about safety, liability, and the potential for hallucination in high-stakes medical contexts. The rollout signals OpenAI's ambition to move beyond general-purpose AI toward specialized, vertical applications that integrate deeply with sensitive personal data ecosystems.
[**The Verge AI**](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/970115/openai-chatgpt-health-launch-claims)

articles.title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
articles.summary:
OpenAI has introduced "Health in ChatGPT," a new feature allowing eligible users in the United States to integrate their personal medical records and Apple Health data directly into the AI interface. This integration enables the model to provide highly personalized health insights and help users better interpret complex medical data. From a strategic perspective, this move signals OpenAI's intent to move beyond general-purpose assistance into specialized, high-stakes domains like healthcare. By leveraging secure data connections, the company is attempting to solve the context gap in AI health advice, shifting from generic information to patient-specific analysis. This development underscores a broader trend of AI integration into the healthcare ecosystem, though it will likely invite increased scrutiny regarding data privacy, HIPAA compliance, and the clinical accuracy of AI-generated health interpretations.
[**OpenAI Blog**](https://openai.com/index/health-in-chatgpt)

articles.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API:  background tasks, remote MCP and more
articles.summary:
Google has expanded the capabilities of Managed Agents within the Gemini API, introducing critical updates to enhance agent autonomy and integration. A primary addition is the support for background tasks, allowing agents to execute long-running processes asynchronously without requiring a constant active connection from the user. Furthermore, the integration of remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers enables agents to connect to external data sources and tools more efficiently, reducing the overhead for developers to maintain local infrastructure. These enhancements shift Gemini from a reactive chat interface toward a proactive agentic framework capable of complex, multi-step workflows. By decoupling task execution from the immediate request-response cycle, Google is positioning its API to support more sophisticated enterprise automation and autonomous operational tasks.
[**Google AI Blog**](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/expanding-managed-agents-gemini-api/)

articles.title: U.S., other nations back open-source AI with 'strong security' at China summit
articles.summary:
At a recent APEC summit, the United States and several other nations reached a consensus to support the development of open-source artificial intelligence, provided it is coupled with robust security frameworks. This agreement marks a significant diplomatic milestone, as it is the first time open-source cooperation has been formally integrated into a ministerial-level statement. The move suggests a strategic shift toward balancing the democratization of AI technology with the necessity of mitigating systemic risks and preventing misuse by adversarial actors. By endorsing open-source models, these nations aim to foster global innovation and transparency while maintaining strict oversight. This development highlights the ongoing tension between the desire for open collaboration and the imperative of national security in the global AI arms race.
[**CNBC Finance**](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/24/china-ai-open-source-apec.html)

articles.title: Thomson Reuters and KKR Announce Joint Venture for Thomson Reuters Global Print Business
articles.summary:
Thomson Reuters has entered into a definitive agreement with KKR to establish a joint venture focused on its global print business. Under the terms of the transaction, Thomson Reuters will divest a 51% stake in the print division to the investment firm. This strategic move allows Thomson Reuters to optimize its capital allocation and accelerate its transition toward a digital-first, AI-driven business model by offloading legacy print assets while retaining a minority interest. For KKR, the venture represents an opportunity to leverage operational efficiencies in a mature media segment. This transaction underscores a broader industry trend where legacy information providers decouple traditional print operations from high-growth digital platforms to improve margins and agility in a rapidly evolving information economy.
[**Reuters Business**](https://ir.thomsonreuters.com/news-releases/news-release-details/thomson-reuters-and-kkr-announce-joint-venture-thomson-reuters)

articles.title: US hits 60 countries with new duties as Trump rebuilds tariff wall
articles.summary:
The United States administration has implemented new import duties targeting 60 countries, signaling a strategic shift toward a more aggressive trade posture. These measures are specifically framed as a response to forced labor investigations, a tactical pivot following a Supreme Court ruling that invalidated previous blanket levies. By tying tariffs to labor standards and specific probes, the administration seeks to bypass legal hurdles while effectively rebuilding a "tariff wall" to protect domestic interests and exert geopolitical pressure. This move is likely to disrupt global supply chains and increase volatility in international trade relations, as affected nations may retaliate with their own trade barriers. The strategy underscores a broader trend of using trade policy as a primary tool for foreign policy and national security objectives.
[**Financial Times**](https://www.ft.com/content/078269b4-5518-4289-b90a-dd722fd7e703?syn-25a6b1a6=1)

articles.title: Intel posts fastest growth in 15 years as AI data centres demand fuels sales
articles.summary:
Intel has reported its most significant growth trajectory in 15 years, driven primarily by a 25% surge in second-quarter revenue. This acceleration is attributed to the escalating demand for AI-capable infrastructure within data centers, as enterprises transition toward generative AI workloads. The results indicate that a strategic pivot toward AI-optimized silicon is beginning to yield tangible financial returns, offsetting previous headwinds in the consumer PC market. While Intel continues to face intense competition from Nvidia and AMD, the current growth suggests a recovery in its data center group and a successful alignment with the broader AI hardware supercycle. This performance underscores the critical role of specialized compute capacity in sustaining the current AI expansion, positioning Intel to recapture market share in the high-performance computing sector.
[**Financial Times**](https://www.ft.com/content/48f0a410-daae-420b-93fc-216c3a2f1184?syn-25a6b1a6=1)

articles.title: AMD’s rivalry with Nvidia is increasingly moving into a new realm
articles.summary:
The strategic competition between AMD and Nvidia is evolving beyond the traditional GPU landscape, expanding significantly into the high-margin server CPU market. While Nvidia has maintained a dominant position in AI accelerators, its push into CPU architecture aims to create a more integrated data center ecosystem, reducing reliance on third-party processors. Conversely, AMD is leveraging its established EPYC server CPU presence to challenge Nvidia's hegemony, attempting to offer a more balanced compute portfolio. This shift indicates a broader industry trend toward heterogeneous computing, where the boundary between general-purpose processing and specialized acceleration blurs. For enterprise clients, this rivalry likely means accelerated innovation cycles and more competitive pricing for data center infrastructure as both firms vie for total control over the AI-driven server stack.
[**MarketWatch**](https://www.marketwatch.com/story/amds-rivalry-with-nvidia-is-increasingly-moving-into-a-new-realm-ac63eceb?mod=mw_rss_topstories)

articles.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
articles.summary:
President Donald Trump has explicitly linked the provision of US assistance for Saudi Arabia's civilian nuclear energy program to the Kingdom's formal recognition of Israel. By conditioning a landmark nuclear deal on Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords, the US administration is leveraging energy security and technological advancement to drive a strategic diplomatic shift in the Middle East. This transactional approach aims to solidify a regional security bloc against common adversaries, specifically Iran, while integrating Saudi Arabia into a broader framework of normalization with Israel. For the Saudi government, this presents a complex geopolitical trade-off between achieving energy diversification goals and managing domestic and regional political sensitivities regarding Palestinian statehood and Islamic leadership. The outcome of these negotiations will significantly impact the geopolitical architecture of the region and US strategic influence.
[**BBC World News**](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cwye71yq8wwo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)

articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Rejected a U.S. Cease-Fire Offer Delivered by Iraq, Officials Say
articles.summary:
Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have escalated following Iran's rejection of a U.S.-proposed cease-fire, which was delivered via the Prime Minister of Iraq. This diplomatic failure signals a hardening of positions between Washington and Tehran, limiting the immediate prospects for a negotiated settlement. In response to the impasse, the U.S. military has maintained its operational tempo, executing its 13th consecutive night of targeted strikes within Iranian territory. This sustained military campaign underscores a strategy of attrition and pressure intended to force a change in Iranian policy. For global markets, this prolonged conflict increases the risk of volatility in energy prices and disrupts regional stability, as the lack of a diplomatic off-ramp suggests a protracted period of kinetic engagement and heightened security risks across the Persian Gulf.
[**New York Times**](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/07/23/world/iran-war-strikes-oil-trump)
